steps.title: Identify the complex series structure
steps.content: The sentence lists three behavioral economists, each identified by name and position/affiliation (an appositive). The blank must mark the boundary between the first and second person, where each item already contains internal commas.

steps.title: Apply the rule for complex series
steps.content: Semicolons—not commas—separate items in a series when the items themselves contain commas. Choice C correctly places a semicolon after “University” to separate Ai Hisano from George Akerlof, keeping the comma after “Akerlof” to mark the appositive. The others fail to properly mark the boundary between series items. That matches choice C — C is correct.
